page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-72-lssd is a virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is configured with 288.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
This server configuration does not include any GPU accelerators, with a GPU count of 0.0. The local storage size is specified as None, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is classified as a general-purpose resource. While benchmark data is currently unavailable for this specific configuration, its hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring substantial multi-core processing capacity alongside proportional memory allocation, such as web serving, application servers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-72-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a family, featuring 72 dedicated vCPUs and 288.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include GPU hardware, and the local storage capacity is designated as None. Additionally, it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Lacking specific benchmark data, its resource density suggests suitability for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded processing, and web serving workloads that require high CPU core counts without specialized memory or storage acceleration.
